[[Billy Joe Dupree]] (uncle) }} }} '''Tyler Dupree''' (born 8 February 2000) is an English professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as {{rlp|pr}} forward for [[Castleford Tigers]] in the [[Super League]] and {{rlnt|England}} at international level. He previously spent time on loan from the [[Leeds Rhinos]] at the [[York City Knights]], [[Featherstone Rovers]] and the [[Batley Bulldogs]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]]. Dupree has also played for [[Oldham RLFC|Oldham]] and the [[Widnes Vikings]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]], and [[Salford Red Devils]] in the [[Super League]].[https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/salford-red-devils-sign-tyler-dupree-in-swap-with-widnes/ Salford Red Devils sign Tyler Dupree in swap with Widnes][https://www.totalrl.com/dupree-bounces-back-from-leeds-rejection-to-make-salford-impact/ Tyler Dupree opens up on painful Leeds Rhinos rejection and journey to Salford Red Devils] ==Background== Born in [[Halifax, West Yorkshire]], Dupree played junior [[rugby league]] for [[Siddal A.R.L.F.C.|Siddal]], and was part of the Halifax Elite Rugby Academy. At the age of 16, he signed scholarship forms with [[Salford Red Devils]] before later joining the academy at [[Leeds Rhinos]].{{cite web |title=Oldham snap up Leeds young gun Dupree |url=https://www.oldham-chronicle.co.uk/news-features/158/roughyeds/135995/oldham-snap-up-leeds-young-gun-dupree |website=Oldham Chronicle |access-date=26 July 2023 |date=14 August 2020}} Dupree played at youth level for the Yorkshire origin and England Academy sides whilst at Leeds.[https://www.yorkpress.co.uk/sport/17646889.york-city-knights-sign-liam-harris-hull-fc-tyler-dupree-leeds-rhinos-harry-aldous-newcastle-thunder/ York City Knights sign Liam Harris from Hull FC, Tyler Dupree from Leeds Rhinos and Harry Aldous from Newcastle Thunder] ==Playing career== ===Leeds Rhinos=== Dupree spent time on loan from Leeds in the [[RFL Championship]] with [[Featherstone Rovers]], [[York City Knights]] and [[Batley Bulldogs]]. After being released by Leeds without making a first team appearance, Dupree considered quitting the sport.{{cite web |title="I was ready to join the Army" How Tyler Dupree was nearly lost to the game |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/tyler-dupree-england-debut-army |website=Love Rugby League |access-date=26 July 2023 |date=28 April 2023}} ===Oldham RLFC=== In August 2020, Dupree signed a one-year deal with [[Oldham RLFC]]. He scored 10 tries in 18 appearances, making him the club's top try scorer during the 2021 season. Despite the club's relegation from the [[RFL Championship|Championship]], he received the Championship Young Player of the Year award in recognition of his performances.{{cite web |title=Dupree picks up Young Player of the Year award |url=https://www.oldham-chronicle.co.uk/news-features/158/roughyeds/143192/dupree-picks-up-young-player-of-the-year-award |website=Oldham Evening Chronicle |access-date=26 July 2023 |date=1 October 2021}} He subsequently joined [[Widnes Vikings]]. ===Salford Red Devils=== In May 2022, Dupree was signed by [[Salford Red Devils|Salford]] in an exchange deal which saw [[Josh Johnson (rugby league)|Josh Johnson]] transfer from Salford to Widnes.{{cite web |title=Tyler Dupree: Salford Red Devils sign Widnes prop as Josh Johnson moves in opposite direction |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/61406334 |website=BBC Sport |access-date=26 July 2023 |date=11 May 2022}}{{Cite web |last=McAllister |first=Josh |date=9 May 2022 |title=Salford Red Devils sign Tyler Dupree in swap with Widnes |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/salford-red-devils-sign-tyler-dupree-in-swap-with-widnes |access-date=27 April 2026 |website=Love Rugby League}} He made his [[Super League]] debut against his former club, the [[Leeds Rhinos]].[https://www.yorkshirepost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/salford-23-leeds-rhinos-8-no-winning-start-for-rohan-smith-3694710 Salford 23 Leeds Rhinos 8: No winning start for Rohan Smith]{{Cite web |last=Ibbetson |first=Stephen |date=29 May 2022 |title=Tyler Dupree opens up on painful Leeds Rhinos rejection and journey to Salford Red Devils |url=https://www.totalrl.com/dupree-bounces-back-from-leeds-rejection-to-make-salford-impact/ |access-date=27 April 2026 |website=TotalRL}} ===Wigan Warriors=== In May 2023, Dupree submitted a transfer request,{{cite web |title=Salford Red Devils rocked as star forward Tyler Dupree hands in transfer request |url=https://www.examinerlive.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/salford-tyler-dupree-transfer-request-26967150 |website=Examiner Live |access-date=26 July 2023 |date=22 May 2023}} which was rejected by Salford.{{cite web |title=Salford Red Devils reject Tyler Dupree transfer request amid serious interest in England prop |url=https://www.yorkshirepost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/salford-red-devils-reject-tyler-dupree-transfer-request-amid-serious-interest-in-england-prop-4153230 |website=Yorkshire Post |access-date=26 July 2023 |date=23 May 2023}} In July 2023, Dupree was signed by [[Wigan Warriors]] for an undisclosed fee, with [[Brad Singleton]] moving from Wigan to Salford as part of the deal.{{cite web |title=Tyler Dupree: Wigan Warriors sign Salford Red Devils prop in swap deal with Brad Singleton |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/66313632 |website=BBC Sport |access-date=26 July 2023 |date=26 July 2023}} In round 20 of the 2023 Super League season, Dupree made his Wigan debut and scored a try in the club's 44–18 victory over rivals Leigh.{{cite news|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2023/jul/29/wigan-leigh-super-league-rugby-league-match-report/|title=Abbas Miski's hat-trick leads mauling as Wigan leapfrog Leigh in Super League|newspaper=The Observer|date=29 July 2023 |last1=Bower |first1=Aaron }} On 14 October 2023, Dupree played in Wigan's [[2023 Super League Grand Final]] victory over the [[Catalans Dragons]].{{cite news|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2023/oct/14/catalans-dragons-wigan-super-league-grand-final-rugby-league-match-report/|title=Wigan Warriors crowned Super League champions after downing Dragons|newspaper=The Observer|date=14 October 2023 |last1=Bower |first1=Aaron }} On 12 October 2024, Dupree played in Wigan's 9-2 2024 Super League grand final victory over Hull Kingston Rovers.{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/live/2024/oct/12/wigan-v-hull-kr-super-league-grand-final-2024-live-score-updates?page=with:block-670aaacb8f087ed1d5a8a350/|title=Wigan v Hull KR: Super League Grand Final 2024 – as it happened|website=www.theguardian.com}} On 1 March 2025, Dupree became the first Super League player to score a try at the [[Rugby League Las Vegas]] event in Wigan's 48–24 win against [[Warrington Wolves]].{{cite web |title=Tyler Dupree creates Super League history with incredible Las Vegas moment |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/tyler-dupree-creates-super-league-history-with-incredible-las-vegas-moment |website=Love Rugby League |access-date=2 March 2025 |date=1 March 2025}} ====Toulouse Olympique (loan)==== On 4 November 2025 it was reported that he would join [[Toulouse Olympique]] on season-long loan starting in 2026{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=4 Nov 2025|author=Aaron Bower|title=Matt Peet explains Tyler Dupree exit as Wigan prop makes Super League switch|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/matt-peet-explains-tyler-dupree-exit-as-wigan-prop-makes-super-league-switch}} ===Castleford Tigers=== On 27 April 2026 it was reported his loan spell at [[Toulouse Olympique|Toulouse]] had ended early, and that he would join [[Castleford Tigers]] on a 3½ year deal{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=27 Apr 2026|author=Aaron Bower|title=Castleford seal immediate Tyler Dupree deal as part of double transfer boost|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/castleford-tigers-tyler-dupree-transfer}} ===International=== In April 2023, Dupree made his international debut for [[England national rugby league team|England]] against [[France national rugby league team|France]]. ==Personal life== Dupree's uncle [[Billy Joe Dupree]] played [[American football]] for the [[Dallas Cowboys]], with whom he won a [[Super Bowl]].[https://www.therhinos.co.uk/player/tyler-dupree/ Leeds Rhinos profile] ==Honours== ===Wigan Warriors=== *'''[[Super League Grand Final|Super League]]''' **'''Winners (2):''' [[2023 Super League Grand Final|2023]], [[2024 Super League Grand Final|2024]] *'''[[League Leaders' Shield]]''' **'''Winners (2):''' [[Super League XXIII|2023]], [[Super League XXIV|2024]] *'''[[World Club Challenge]]''' **'''Winners (1):''' [[2024 World Club Challenge|2024]] ===Individual=== *'''[[RFL Championship|Championship]] Young Player of the Year''' **'''Winners (1):''' [[2021 RFL Championship|2021]] ==References== {{reflist|30em}} ==External links==
